title: Supersede: metadata inheritance + propagation sweep + capture stubs (D2/D6)
action: Per CONTEXT D2 and D6: `decisions supersede` accepts --tags/--scope, inheriting BOTH from the superseded target when omitted (target metadata-less -> scope falls back to the target's scope or 'repo'). After writing the supersede event, run the propagation sweep: recursively scan docs/** text files (md/json/yaml/txt) for the superseded id — full uuid AND short8 with word-boundary regex (no hex false positives) — collect {file, line, excerpt<=160ch}. Print the hit list in the human output and --json payload; store a `sweep` summary {scanned_at, hit_count, files[]} on the supersede event; append one capture stub per hit via the capture module (source "supersede-sweep", outcome naming file:line + the new decision id) so unreconciled hits surface at every flush. Zero hits prints a clean no-citations line. Mirror to .bee/bin, extend test_decisions_propagation.mjs (inheritance, sweep hit/no-hit, short8 word-boundary negative, stub creation), render + manifest.

truths: supersede inherits tags/scope from target when flags omitted
sweep finds full-id and short8 citations under docs/** and writes one stub per hit
short8 embedded in a longer hex string does NOT match
prohibitions: Sweep never edits the citing files itself
Sweep scope is docs/** only (CONTEXT pinned; .bee/spikes excluded)
